io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n162\t\tif err != nil {\n163\t\t\treturn nil, err\n164\t\t}\n165\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n166\t\n167\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n168\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n169\t\t\treturn nil, err\n170\t\t}\n171\t\n172\t\treturn &ErrorResponseFixture{\n173\t\t\tS3Error:   s3Err,\n174\t\t\tHTTPStatus: resp.StatusCode,\n175\t\t\tHeaders:   resp.Header.Clone(),\n176\t\t\tBody:      body,\n177\t\t\tDuration:  duration,\n178\t\t}, nil\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t// =============================================================================\n182\t// ERROR CATEGORY ENUMERATION\n183\t// =============================================================================\n184\t\n185\t// ErrorCategory represents different categories of errors.\n186\ttype ErrorCategory string\n187\t\n188\tconst (\n189\t\t// CategoryAuth authentication errors (401, 403)\n190\t\tCategoryAuth ErrorCategory = \"Auth\"\n191\t\n192\t\t// CategoryNotFound resource not found errors (404)\n193\t\tCategoryNotFound ErrorCategory = \"NotFound\"\n194\t\n195\t\t// CategoryInvalidRequest invalid request errors (400, 415)\n196\t\tCategoryInvalidRequest ErrorCategory = \"InvalidRequest\"\n197\t\n198\t\t// CategoryMethodNotAllowed method not allowed errors (405)\n199\t\tCategoryMethodNotAllowed ErrorCategory = \"MethodNotAllowed\"\n200\t\n201\t\t// CategoryInternal server errors (500)\n202\t\tCategoryInternal ErrorCategory = \"Internal\"\n203\t\n204\t\t// CategoryCORS CORS-related errors\n205\t\tCategoryCORS ErrorCategory = \"CORS\"\n206\t\n207\t\t// CategoryGeneral general uncategorized errors\n208\t\tCategoryGeneral ErrorCategory = \"General\"\n209\t)\n210\t\n211\t// String returns the string representation of the error category.\n212\tfunc (e ErrorCategory) String() string {\n213\t\treturn string(e)\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\t// =============================================================================\n217\t// ERROR CODE CONSTANTS\n218\t// =============================================================================\n219\t\n220\t// Common S3 error codes used across testing.\n221\tconst (\n222\t\t// ErrorCodeAccessDenied - Access denied (403)\n223\t\tErrorCodeAccessDenied = \"AccessDenied\"\n224\t\n225\t\t// ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId - Invalid access key (403)\n226\t\tErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId = \"InvalidAccessKeyId\"\n227\t\n228\t\t// ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch - Signature mismatch (403)\n229\t\tErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch = \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\"\n230\t\n231\t\t// ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken - Missing auth header (403)\n232\t\tErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken = \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"\n233\t\n234\t\t// ErrorCodeNoSuchKey - Resource not found (404)\n235\t\tErrorCodeNoSuchKey = \"NoSuchKey\"\n236\t\n237\t\t// ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed - Method not allowed (405)\n238\t\tErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed = \"MethodNotAllowed\"\n239\t\n240\t\t// ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType - Unsupported media type (415)\n241\t\tErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType = \"UnsupportedMediaType\"\n242\t\n243\t\t// ErrorCodeInvalidRequest - Invalid request (400)\n244\t\tErrorCodeInvalidRequest = \"InvalidRequest\"\n245\t\n246\t\t// ErrorCodeInternalError - Internal server error (500)\n247\t\tErrorCodeInternalError = \"InternalError\"\n248\t\n249\t\t// ErrorCodeRequestExpired - Request timestamp expired (403)\n250\t\tErrorCodeRequestExpired = \"RequestExpired\"\n251\t)\n252\t\n253\t// =============================================================================\n254\t// ERROR CATEGORY MAPPING\n255\t// =============================================================================\n256\t\n257\t// CategoryForCode returns the error category for a given error code.\n258\tfunc CategoryForCode(code string) ErrorCategory {\n259\t\tswitch code {\n260\t\tcase ErrorCodeAccessDenied, ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId,\n261\t\t     ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch, ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n262\t\t     ErrorCodeRequestExpired:\n263\t\t\treturn CategoryAuth\n264\t\tcase ErrorCodeNoSuchKey:\n265\t\t\treturn CategoryNotFound\n266\t\tcase ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed:\n267\t\t\treturn CategoryMethodNotAllowed\n268\t\tcase ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType, ErrorCodeInvalidRequest:\n269\t\t\treturn CategoryInvalidRequest\n270\t\tcase ErrorCodeInternalError:\n271\t\t\treturn CategoryInternal\n272\t\tdefault:\n273\t\t\treturn CategoryGeneral\n274\t\t}\n275\t}\n276\t\n277\t// =============================================================================\n278\t// HTTP STATUS CODE MAPPING\n279\t// =============================================================================\n280\t\n281\t// ExpectedStatusCodeForCode returns the expected HTTP status code for an S3 error code.\n282\tfunc ExpectedStatusCodeForCode(code string) int {\n283\t\tswitch code {\n284\t\tcase ErrorCodeAccessDenied, ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId,\n285\t\t     ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch, ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n286\t\t     ErrorCodeRequestExpired:\n287\t\t\treturn 403\n288\t\tcase ErrorCodeNoSuchKey:\n289\t\t\treturn 404\n290\t\tcase ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed:\n291\t\t\treturn 405\n292\t\tcase ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType:\n293\t\t\treturn 415\n294\t\tcase ErrorCodeInvalidRequest:\n295\t\t\treturn 400\n296\t\tcase ErrorCodeInternalError:\n297\t\t\treturn 500\n298\t\tdefault:\n299\t\t\treturn 500 // Default to internal error for unknown codes\n300\t\t}\n301\t}\n302\t\n303\t// =============================================================================\n304\t// PREDEFINED COMMON ERROR PATTERNS\n305\t// =============================================================================\n306\t// These predefined patterns represent common error scenarios that can be reused\n307\t// across different test files. Each pattern is fully documented and provides\n308\t// a complete ErrorScenarioConfig ready for use.\n309\t//\n310\t// Usage Examples:\n311\t//   // Use a predefined pattern directly\n312\t//   pattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n313\t//   config := pattern.Config()\n314\t//\n315\t//   // Extend a predefined pattern\n316\t//   customPattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n317\t//   customPattern.Config.ExpectedMessage = \"Custom not found message\"\n318\t//\n319\t//   // Create a new pattern based on a predefined one\n320\t//   myPattern := ErrorScenarioConfig{\n321\t//       Name: \"My custom pattern\",\n322\t//       ExpectedCode: CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound.Config.ExpectedCode,\n323\t//       ExpectedStatus: CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound.Config.ExpectedStatus,\n324\t//   }\n325\t// =============================================================================\n326\t\n327\t// CommonErrorPatterns provides a collection of predefined error patterns\n328\t// for common S3 error scenarios. These patterns are ready-to-use and cover\n329\t// the most frequent error cases encountered in testing.\n330\tvar CommonErrorPatterns = struct {\n331\t\t// ResourceNotFound covers the classic 404 error when a requested object does not exist\n332\t\tResourceNotFound ErrorScenarioConfig\n333\t\n334\t\t// AccessDenied covers authentication failures where credentials are invalid\n335\t\tAccessDenied ErrorScenarioConfig\n336\t\n337\t\t// InvalidRequest covers malformed requests with missing or invalid parameters\n338\t\tInvalidRequest ErrorScenarioConfig\n339\t\n340\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType covers content-type validation errors\n341\t\tUnsupportedMediaType ErrorScenarioConfig\n342\t\n343\t\t// MethodNotAllowed covers HTTP method validation errors\n344\t\tMethodNotAllowed ErrorScenarioConfig\n345\t\n346\t\t// InternalServerError covers server-side errors\n347\t\tInternalServerError ErrorScenarioConfig\n348\t\n349\t\t// SignatureMismatch covers AWS signature validation failures\n350\t\tSignatureMismatch ErrorScenarioConfig\n351\t\n352\t\t// RequestExpired covers timestamp validation errors\n353\t\tRequestExpired ErrorScenarioConfig\n354\t}{\n355\t\t// ResourceNotFound: Standard 404 error for missing objects\n356\t\tResourceNotFound: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n357\t\t\tName:              \"Resource Not Found\",\n358\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeNoSuchKey,\n359\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    404,\n360\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The specified key does not exist\",\n361\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"not\", \"found\", \"exist\", \"no such key\"},\n362\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n363\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    500 * time.Millisecond,\n364\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests attempting to access non-existent resources. Covers GET, HEAD, and other operations on missing objects.\",\n365\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryNotFound),\n366\t\t},\n367\t\n368\t\t// AccessDenied: Authentication failure with invalid credentials\n369\t\tAccessDenied: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n370\t\t\tName:              \"Access Denied\",\n371\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeAccessDenied,\n372\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n373\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Access Denied\",\n374\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"access\", \"denied\", \"permission\", \"authorized\"},\n375\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   12,\n376\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n377\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with invalid or missing credentials. Covers both missing Authorization headers and invalid access keys.\",\n378\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n379\t\t},\n380\t\n381\t\t// InvalidRequest: Malformed request parameters\n382\t\tInvalidRequest: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n383\t\t\tName:              \"Invalid Request\",\n384\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n385\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    400,\n386\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The request is invalid\",\n387\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"invalid\", \"request\", \"malformed\"},\n388\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n389\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n390\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with missing required parameters, invalid query strings, or malformed request bodies.\",\n391\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInvalidRequest),\n392\t\t},\n393\t\n394\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType: Content-type validation error\n395\t\tUnsupportedMediaType: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n396\t\t\tName:              \"Unsupported Media Type\",\n397\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType,\n398\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    415,\n399\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The content type is not supported\",\n400\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"content\", \"type\", \"supported\", \"media\"},\n401\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n402\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n403\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with unsupported Content-Type headers. Used when the server requires specific content types (e.g., application/xml).\",\n404\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInvalidRequest),\n405\t\t},\n406\t\n407\t\t// MethodNotAllowed: HTTP method validation error\n408\t\tMethodNotAllowed: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n409\t\t\tName:              \"Method Not Allowed\",\n410\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed,\n411\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    405,\n412\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The specified method is not allowed\",\n413\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"method\", \"allowed\", \"supported\"},\n414\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n415\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n416\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests using HTTP methods that are not supported for the given resource. Commonly tested with POST on object endpoints.\",\n417\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryMethodNotAllowed),\n418\t\t},\n419\t\n420\t\t// InternalServerError: Server-side errors\n421\t\tInternalServerError: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n422\t\t\tName:              \"Internal Server Error\",\n423\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInternalError,\n424\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    500,\n425\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Internal server error\",\n426\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"internal\", \"error\", \"server\"},\n427\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n428\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    1000 * time.Millisecond,\n429\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for unexpected server-side errors. These typically indicate a bug or infrastructure issue that needs investigation.\",\n430\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInternal),\n431\t\t},\n432\t\n433\t\t// SignatureMismatch: AWS signature validation failure\n434\t\tSignatureMismatch: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n435\t\t\tName:              \"Signature Does Not Match\",\n436\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch,\n437\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n438\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The request signature we calculated does not match\",\n439\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"signature\", \"match\", \"calculated\"},\n440\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   25,\n441\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n442\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with incorrect AWS signature calculations. Covers malformed signatures, tampered requests, and incorrect secret keys.\",\n443\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n444\t\t},\n445\t\n446\t\t// RequestExpired: Timestamp validation error\n447\t\tRequestExpired: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n448\t\t\tName:              \"Request Expired\",\n449\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeRequestExpired,\n450\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n451\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Request has expired\",\n452\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"expired\", \"timestamp\", \"date\"},\n453\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n454\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n455\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with expired timestamps. AWS S3 requires requests to be made within a specific time window (typically 15 minutes).\",\n456\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n457\t\t},\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\t// =============================================================================\n461\t// AUTHENTICATION ERROR PATTERNS\n462\t// =============================================================================\n463\t// These patterns are specifically designed for authentication-related testing.\n464\t// They cover the spectrum of authentication failures from missing credentials\n465\t// to expired timestamps.\n466\t// =============================================================================\n467\t\n468\t// AuthErrorPatterns provides predefined patterns for authentication error scenarios.\n469\tvar AuthErrorPatterns = struct {\n470\t\t// MissingAuthHeader covers requests without Authorization header\n471\t\tMissingAuthHeader ErrorScenarioConfig\n472\t\n473\t\t// InvalidAccessKeyId covers requests with invalid access key\n474\t\tInvalidAccessKeyId ErrorScenarioConfig\n475\t\n476\t\t// SignatureDoesNotMatch covers requests with incorrect signature\n477\t\tSignatureDoesNotMatch ErrorScenarioConfig\n478\t\n479\t\t// MissingDateHeader covers requests without X-Amz-Date header\n480\t\tMissingDateHeader ErrorScenarioConfig\n481\t\n482\t\t// RequestExpired covers requests with expired timestamp\n483\t\tRequestExpired ErrorScenarioConfig\n484\t\n485\t\t// MalformedAuthHeader covers requests with invalid Authorization header format\n486\t\tMalformedAuthHeader ErrorScenarioConfig\n487\t}{\n488\t\t// MissingAuthHeader: No Authorization header present\n489\t\tMissingAuthHeader: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n490\t\t\tName:              \"Missing Authentication Token\",\n491\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n492\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n493\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Missing Authentication Token\",\n494\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"missing\", \"authentication\", \"token\", \"authorization\"},\n495\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n496\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    250 * time.Millisecond,\n497\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests completely missing the Authorization header. This is the most basic authentication failure scenario.\",\n498\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n499\t\t},\n500\t\n501\t\t// InvalidAccessKeyId: Access key not found or invalid\n502\t\tInvalidAccessKeyId: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n503\t\t\tName:              \"Invalid Access Key ID\",\n504\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId,\n505\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n506\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records\",\n507\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"access\", \"key\", \"invalid\", \"exist\"},\n508\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n509\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n510\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with an invalid or non-existent access key ID. This typically means the key was mistyped or does not belong to any account.\",\n511\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n512\t\t},\n513\t\n514\t\t// SignatureDoesNotMatch: Calculated signature doesn't match\n515\t\tSignatureDoesNotMatch: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n516\t\t\tName:              \"Signature Does Not Match\",\n517\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch,\n518\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n519\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\",\n520\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"signature\", \"match\", \"calculated\", \"provided\"},\n521\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   30,\n522\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    350 * time.Millisecond,\n523\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests where the signature calculation failed. This can be due to incorrect secret key, tampered request, or signing algorithm mismatch.\",\n524\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n525\t\t},\n526\t\n527\t\t// MissingDateHeader: Required date header is missing\n528\t\tMissingDateHeader: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n529\t\t\tName:              \"Missing Date Header\",\n530\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n531\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n532\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Missing required date header\",\n533\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"missing\", \"date\", \"header\", \"timestamp\"},\n534\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n535\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    250 * time.Millisecond,\n536\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests missing the X-Amz-Date header. The date header is required for signature calculation and request timestamp validation.\",\n537\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n538\t\t},\n539\t\n540\t\t// RequestExpired: Request timestamp is too old\n541\t\tRequestExpired: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n542\t\t\tName:              \"Request Expired\",\n543\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeRequestExpired,\n544\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n545\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Request has expired\",\n546\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"expired\", \"timestamp\", \"date\", \"old\"},\n547\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n548\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n549\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with timestamps outside the valid window (typically more than 15 minutes old). AWS S3 rejects stale requests.\",\n550\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n551\t\t},\n552\t\n553\t\t// MalformedAuthHeader: Authorization header format is invalid\n554\t\tMalformedAuthHeader: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n555\t\t\tName:              \"Malformed Authorization Header\",\n556\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n557\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n558\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Invalid authorization header format\",\n559\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"authorization\", \"header\", \"malformed\", \"format\", \"invalid\"},\n560\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n561\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    250 * time.Millisecond,\n562\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with Authorization headers that don't match the expected AWS signature format. Covers missing fields or incorrect structure.\",\n563\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n564\t\t},\n565\t}\n566\t\n567\t// =============================================================================\n568\t// CLIENT ERROR PATTERNS (4xx)\n569\t// =============================================================================\n570\t// These patterns represent client-side errors (4xx status codes). These are\n571\t// errors where the client made an invalid request and should correct their\n572\t// request before retrying.\n573\t// =============================================================================\n574\t\n575\t// ClientErrorPatterns provides predefined patterns for client error scenarios (4xx).\n576\tvar ClientErrorPatterns = struct {\n577\t\t// BadRequest covers generic 400 Bad Request errors\n578\t\tBadRequest ErrorScenarioConfig\n579\t\n580\t\t// NotFound covers 404 Not Found errors\n581\t\tNotFound ErrorScenarioConfig\n582\t\n583\t\t// MethodNotAllowed covers 405 Method Not Allowed errors\n584\t\tMethodNotAllowed ErrorScenarioConfig\n585\t\n586\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType covers 415 Unsupported Media Type errors\n587\t\tUnsupportedMediaType ErrorScenarioConfig\n588\t}{\n589\t\t// BadRequest: Generic bad request error\n590\t\tBadRequest: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n591\t\t\tName:              \"Bad Request\",\n592\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n593\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    400,\n594\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Bad Request\",\n595\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"bad\", \"request\", \"invalid\"},\n596\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   10,\n597\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n598\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for generic bad request errors. This is a catch-all for malformed requests that don't fit more specific error categories.\",\n599\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInvalidRequest),\n600\t\t},\n601\t\n602\t\t// NotFound: Resource not found\n603\t\tNotFound: CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound,\n604\t\n605\t\t// MethodNotAllowed: HTTP method not supported\n606\t\tMethodNotAllowed: CommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n607\t\n608\t\t// UnsupportedMediaType: Content type not supported\n609\t\tUnsupportedMediaType: CommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n610\t}\n611\t\n612\t// =============================================================================\n613\t// SERVER ERROR PATTERNS (5xx)\n614\t// =============================================================================\n615\t// These patterns represent server-side errors (5xx status codes). These are\n616\t// errors where the server failed to fulfill a valid request.\n617\t// =============================================================================\n618\t\n619\t// ServerErrorPatterns provides predefined patterns for server error scenarios (5xx).\n620\tvar ServerErrorPatterns = struct {\n621\t\t// InternalError covers 500 Internal Server Error\n622\t\tInternalError ErrorScenarioConfig\n623\t\n624\t\t// ServiceUnavailable covers 503 Service Unavailable\n625\t\tServiceUnavailable ErrorScenarioConfig\n626\t}{\n627\t\t// InternalError: Generic internal server error\n628\t\tInternalError: CommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError,\n629\t\n630\t\t// ServiceUnavailable: Service temporarily unavailable\n631\t\tServiceUnavailable: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n632\t\t\tName:              \"Service Unavailable\",\n633\t\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInternalError,\n634\t\t\tExpectedStatus:    503,\n635\t\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Service Unavailable\",\n636\t\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"unavailable\", \"service\", \"temporarily\"},\n637\t\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n638\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:    2000 * time.Millisecond,\n639\t\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for service unavailability errors. This typically occurs during maintenance, overload, or infrastructure issues.\",\n640\t\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInternal),\n641\t\t},\n642\t}\n643\t\n644\t// =============================================================================\n645\t// ERROR PATTERN COLLECTIONS BY CATEGORY\n646\t// =============================================================================\n647\t// These collections organize patterns by error category for easy access\n648\t// when testing specific types of errors.\n649\t// =============================================================================\n650\t\n651\t// ErrorPatternByCategory maps error categories to their predefined patterns.\n652\t// This allows quick access to all patterns for a specific error type.\n653\tvar ErrorPatternByCategory = map[ErrorCategory][]ErrorScenarioConfig{\n654\t\tCategoryAuth: {\n655\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader,\n656\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.InvalidAccessKeyId,\n657\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.SignatureDoesNotMatch,\n658\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.MissingDateHeader,\n659\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.RequestExpired,\n660\t\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.MalformedAuthHeader,\n661\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.SignatureMismatch,\n662\t\t},\n663\t\tCategoryNotFound: {\n664\t\t\tClientErrorPatterns.NotFound,\n665\t\t},\n666\t\tCategoryInvalidRequest: {\n667\t\t\tClientErrorPatterns.BadRequest,\n668\t\t\tClientErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n669\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest,\n670\t\t},\n671\t\tCategoryMethodNotAllowed: {\n672\t\t\tClientErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n673\t\t},\n674\t\tCategoryInternal: {\n675\t\t\tServerErrorPatterns.InternalError,\n676\t\t\tServerErrorPatterns.ServiceUnavailable,\n677\t\t},\n678\t}\n679\t\n680\t// =============================================================================\n681\t// ERROR PATTERN BUILDERS\n682\t// =============================================================================\n683\t// These builder functions help create custom error patterns based on\n684\t// predefined patterns or from scratch.\n685\t// =============================================================================\n686\t\n687\t// PatternForCode returns a predefined pattern for the given error code,\n688\t// or a default pattern if no specific pattern exists.\n689\tfunc PatternForCode(code string) ErrorScenarioConfig {\n690\t\tswitch code {\n691\t\tcase ErrorCodeAccessDenied:\n692\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied\n693\t\tcase ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId:\n694\t\t\treturn AuthErrorPatterns.InvalidAccessKeyId\n695\t\tcase ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch:\n696\t\t\treturn AuthErrorPatterns.SignatureDoesNotMatch\n697\t\tcase ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken:\n698\t\t\treturn AuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader\n699\t\tcase ErrorCodeNoSuchKey:\n700\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n701\t\tcase ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed:\n702\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed\n703\t\tcase ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType:\n704\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType\n705\t\tcase ErrorCodeInvalidRequest:\n706\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest\n707\t\tcase ErrorCodeInternalError:\n708\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError\n709\t\tcase ErrorCodeRequestExpired:\n710\t\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.RequestExpired\n711\t\tdefault:\n712\t\t\t// Return a default pattern for unknown codes\n713\t\t\treturn ErrorScenarioConfig{\n714\t\t\t\tName:             \"Unknown Error Pattern\",\n715\t\t\t\tExpectedCode:     code,\n716\t\t\t\tExpectedStatus:   500,\n717\t\t\t\tExpectedMessage:  \"Unknown error\",\n718\t\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 10,\n719\t\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:  1000 * time.Millisecond,\n720\t\t\t\tDescription:      \"Default pattern for unknown error codes\",\n721\t\t\t\tCategory:         string(CategoryGeneral),\n722\t\t\t}\n723\t\t}\n724\t}\n725\t\n726\t// PatternsForCategory returns all predefined patterns for a given error category.\n727\tfunc PatternsForCategory(category ErrorCategory) []ErrorScenarioConfig {\n728\t\tpatterns, exists := ErrorPatternByCategory[category]\n729\t\tif !exists {\n730\t\t\treturn []ErrorScenarioConfig{}\n731\t\t}\n732\t\tresult := make([]ErrorScenarioConfig, len(patterns))\n733\t\tcopy(result, patterns)\n734\t\treturn result\n735\t}\n736\t\n737\t// AllCommonPatterns returns all predefined common error patterns.\n738\tfunc AllCommonPatterns() []ErrorScenarioConfig {\n739\t\treturn []ErrorScenarioConfig{\n740\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound,\n741\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied,\n742\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest,\n743\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n744\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n745\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError,\n746\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.SignatureMismatch,\n747\t\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.RequestExpired,\n748\t\t}\n749\t}\n750\t\n751\t// server\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TEST PATTERNS - BASE TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n// This file provides foundational types and structures for error response testing.\n// It defines the common error structures that are used across both test and\n// non-test code, ensuring type consistency and reusability.\n//\n// Design Philosophy:\n// - Separation of concerns: base types in this file, test-specific helpers in test files\n// - Type consistency: shared types across test and non-test code\n// - Reusability: fixtures and patterns can be used anywhere\n// - Self-documenting: clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n//\n// Usage:\n//   - Test files import this package for base error structures\n//   - Non-test files (fixtures) use S3Error for type-safe error responses\n//   - Test patterns build on these base types\n// =============================================================================\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CORE ERROR TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// S3Error represents an S3 XML error response.\n// This type is used across error testing, fixtures, and validation.\n// It matches the standard S3 error response format with Code and Message fields.\ntype S3Error struct {\n\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Error\"`\n\tCode    string   `xml:\"Code\"`\n\tMessage string   `xml:\"Message\"`\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR SCENARIO TYPES (for non-test fixture usage)\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorScenarioConfig represents configuration for an error test scenario.\n// This type is used in both test fixtures and non-test code to define\n// expected error behaviors.\ntype ErrorScenarioConfig struct {\n\t// Name is the scenario name for identification\n\tName string\n\n\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code (e.g., \"NoSuchKey\", \"AccessDenied\")\n\tExpectedCode string\n\n\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n\tExpectedStatus int\n\n\t// ExpectedMessage contains text that should appear in the error message\n\tExpectedMessage string\n\n\t// ExpectedKeywords are alternative keywords (any match is acceptable)\n\tExpectedKeywords []string\n\n\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n\tMinMessageLength int\n\n\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n\n\t// Description explains what this scenario tests\n\tDescription string\n\n\t// Category categorizes the error type (e.g., \"NotFound\", \"Auth\", \"CORS\")\n\tCategory string\n}\n\n// DefaultErrorScenarioConfig returns a default scenario configuration.\nfunc DefaultErrorScenarioConfig() ErrorScenarioConfig {\n\treturn ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 15,\n\t\tCategory:         \"General\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR RESPONSE VALIDATION TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorResponseMetadata contains metadata about an error response.\n// This type is used for logging, debugging, and performance monitoring.\ntype ErrorResponseMetadata struct {\n\t// StatusCode is the HTTP status code received\n\tStatusCode int\n\n\t// ErrorCode is the S3 error code parsed from the response\n\tErrorCode string\n\n\t// Message is the error message text\n\tMessage string\n\n\t// ResponseTime is how long the request took\n\tResponseTime time.Duration\n\n\t// ContentLength is the size of the response body\n\tContentLength int64\n\n\t// ContentType is the response content type header\n\tContentType string\n\n\t// Timestamp when the response was received\n\tTimestamp time.Time\n}\n\n// ExtractMetadata extracts metadata from an HTTP response.\n// This helper is useful for logging and debugging error responses.\nfunc ExtractMetadata(resp *http.Response, duration time.Duration) ErrorResponseMetadata {\n\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\txml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err)\n\n\treturn ErrorResponseMetadata{\n\t\tStatusCode:   resp.StatusCode,\n\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n\t\tResponseTime: duration,\n\t\tContentLength: resp.ContentLength,\n\t\tContentType:  resp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"),\n\t\tTimestamp:    time.Now(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR FIXTURE TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorResponseFixture represents a complete error response fixture.\n// This type combines the S3 error with HTTP response metadata for\n// comprehensive testing scenarios.\ntype ErrorResponseFixture struct {\n\t// S3Error is the parsed S3 error structure\n\tS3Error S3Error\n\n\t// HTTPStatus is the HTTP status code\n\tHTTPStatus int\n\n\t// Headers contains the HTTP response headers\n\tHeaders http.Header\n\n\t// Body is the raw response body\n\tBody []byte\n\n\t// Duration is how long the response took\n\tDuration time.Duration\n}\n\n// ToFixture converts an HTTP response to a fixture.\nfunc ToFixture(resp *http.Response, duration time.Duration) (*ErrorResponseFixture, error) {\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ErrorResponseFixture{\n\t\tS3Error:   s3Err,\n\t\tHTTPStatus: resp.StatusCode,\n\t\tHeaders:   resp.Header.Clone(),\n\t\tBody:      body,\n\t\tDuration:  duration,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR CATEGORY ENUMERATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorCategory represents different categories of errors.\ntype ErrorCategory string\n\nconst (\n\t// CategoryAuth authentication errors (401, 403)\n\tCategoryAuth ErrorCategory = \"Auth\"\n\n\t// CategoryNotFound resource not found errors (404)\n\tCategoryNotFound ErrorCategory = \"NotFound\"\n\n\t// CategoryInvalidRequest invalid request errors (400, 415)\n\tCategoryInvalidRequest ErrorCategory = \"InvalidRequest\"\n\n\t// CategoryMethodNotAllowed method not allowed errors (405)\n\tCategoryMethodNotAllowed ErrorCategory = \"MethodNotAllowed\"\n\n\t// CategoryInternal server errors (500)\n\tCategoryInternal ErrorCategory = \"Internal\"\n\n\t// CategoryCORS CORS-related errors\n\tCategoryCORS ErrorCategory = \"CORS\"\n\n\t// CategoryGeneral general uncategorized errors\n\tCategoryGeneral ErrorCategory = \"General\"\n)\n\n// String returns the string representation of the error category.\nfunc (e ErrorCategory) String() string {\n\treturn string(e)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR CODE CONSTANTS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// Common S3 error codes used across testing.\nconst (\n\t// ErrorCodeAccessDenied - Access denied (403)\n\tErrorCodeAccessDenied = \"AccessDenied\"\n\n\t// ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId - Invalid access key (403)\n\tErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId = \"InvalidAccessKeyId\"\n\n\t// ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch - Signature mismatch (403)\n\tErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch = \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\"\n\n\t// ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken - Missing auth header (403)\n\tErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken = \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"\n\n\t// ErrorCodeNoSuchKey - Resource not found (404)\n\tErrorCodeNoSuchKey = \"NoSuchKey\"\n\n\t// ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed - Method not allowed (405)\n\tErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed = \"MethodNotAllowed\"\n\n\t// ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType - Unsupported media type (415)\n\tErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType = \"UnsupportedMediaType\"\n\n\t// ErrorCodeInvalidRequest - Invalid request (400)\n\tErrorCodeInvalidRequest = \"InvalidRequest\"\n\n\t// ErrorCodeInternalError - Internal server error (500)\n\tErrorCodeInternalError = \"InternalError\"\n\n\t// ErrorCodeRequestExpired - Request timestamp expired (403)\n\tErrorCodeRequestExpired = \"RequestExpired\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR CATEGORY MAPPING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// CategoryForCode returns the error category for a given error code.\nfunc CategoryForCode(code string) ErrorCategory {\n\tswitch code {\n\tcase ErrorCodeAccessDenied, ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId,\n\t     ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch, ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n\t     ErrorCodeRequestExpired:\n\t\treturn CategoryAuth\n\tcase ErrorCodeNoSuchKey:\n\t\treturn CategoryNotFound\n\tcase ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed:\n\t\treturn CategoryMethodNotAllowed\n\tcase ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType, ErrorCodeInvalidRequest:\n\t\treturn CategoryInvalidRequest\n\tcase ErrorCodeInternalError:\n\t\treturn CategoryInternal\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn CategoryGeneral\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HTTP STATUS CODE MAPPING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ExpectedStatusCodeForCode returns the expected HTTP status code for an S3 error code.\nfunc ExpectedStatusCodeForCode(code string) int {\n\tswitch code {\n\tcase ErrorCodeAccessDenied, ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId,\n\t     ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch, ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n\t     ErrorCodeRequestExpired:\n\t\treturn 403\n\tcase ErrorCodeNoSuchKey:\n\t\treturn 404\n\tcase ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed:\n\t\treturn 405\n\tcase ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType:\n\t\treturn 415\n\tcase ErrorCodeInvalidRequest:\n\t\treturn 400\n\tcase ErrorCodeInternalError:\n\t\treturn 500\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn 500 // Default to internal error for unknown codes\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// PREDEFINED COMMON ERROR PATTERNS\n// =============================================================================\n// These predefined patterns represent common error scenarios that can be reused\n// across different test files. Each pattern is fully documented and provides\n// a complete ErrorScenarioConfig ready for use.\n//\n// Usage Examples:\n//   // Use a predefined pattern directly\n//   pattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n//   config := pattern.Config()\n//\n//   // Extend a predefined pattern\n//   customPattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n//   customPattern.Config.ExpectedMessage = \"Custom not found message\"\n//\n//   // Create a new pattern based on a predefined one\n//   myPattern := ErrorScenarioConfig{\n//       Name: \"My custom pattern\",\n//       ExpectedCode: CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound.Config.ExpectedCode,\n//       ExpectedStatus: CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound.Config.ExpectedStatus,\n//   }\n// =============================================================================\n\n// CommonErrorPatterns provides a collection of predefined error patterns\n// for common S3 error scenarios. These patterns are ready-to-use and cover\n// the most frequent error cases encountered in testing.\nvar CommonErrorPatterns = struct {\n\t// ResourceNotFound covers the classic 404 error when a requested object does not exist\n\tResourceNotFound ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// AccessDenied covers authentication failures where credentials are invalid\n\tAccessDenied ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// InvalidRequest covers malformed requests with missing or invalid parameters\n\tInvalidRequest ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// UnsupportedMediaType covers content-type validation errors\n\tUnsupportedMediaType ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// MethodNotAllowed covers HTTP method validation errors\n\tMethodNotAllowed ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// InternalServerError covers server-side errors\n\tInternalServerError ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// SignatureMismatch covers AWS signature validation failures\n\tSignatureMismatch ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// RequestExpired covers timestamp validation errors\n\tRequestExpired ErrorScenarioConfig\n}{\n\t// ResourceNotFound: Standard 404 error for missing objects\n\tResourceNotFound: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Resource Not Found\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeNoSuchKey,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    404,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The specified key does not exist\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"not\", \"found\", \"exist\", \"no such key\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    500 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests attempting to access non-existent resources. Covers GET, HEAD, and other operations on missing objects.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryNotFound),\n\t},\n\n\t// AccessDenied: Authentication failure with invalid credentials\n\tAccessDenied: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Access Denied\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeAccessDenied,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Access Denied\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"access\", \"denied\", \"permission\", \"authorized\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   12,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with invalid or missing credentials. Covers both missing Authorization headers and invalid access keys.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n\t},\n\n\t// InvalidRequest: Malformed request parameters\n\tInvalidRequest: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Invalid Request\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    400,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The request is invalid\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"invalid\", \"request\", \"malformed\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with missing required parameters, invalid query strings, or malformed request bodies.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInvalidRequest),\n\t},\n\n\t// UnsupportedMediaType: Content-type validation error\n\tUnsupportedMediaType: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Unsupported Media Type\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    415,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The content type is not supported\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"content\", \"type\", \"supported\", \"media\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with unsupported Content-Type headers. Used when the server requires specific content types (e.g., application/xml).\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInvalidRequest),\n\t},\n\n\t// MethodNotAllowed: HTTP method validation error\n\tMethodNotAllowed: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Method Not Allowed\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    405,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The specified method is not allowed\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"method\", \"allowed\", \"supported\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests using HTTP methods that are not supported for the given resource. Commonly tested with POST on object endpoints.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryMethodNotAllowed),\n\t},\n\n\t// InternalServerError: Server-side errors\n\tInternalServerError: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Internal Server Error\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInternalError,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    500,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Internal server error\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"internal\", \"error\", \"server\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    1000 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for unexpected server-side errors. These typically indicate a bug or infrastructure issue that needs investigation.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInternal),\n\t},\n\n\t// SignatureMismatch: AWS signature validation failure\n\tSignatureMismatch: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Signature Does Not Match\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The request signature we calculated does not match\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"signature\", \"match\", \"calculated\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   25,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with incorrect AWS signature calculations. Covers malformed signatures, tampered requests, and incorrect secret keys.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n\t},\n\n\t// RequestExpired: Timestamp validation error\n\tRequestExpired: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Request Expired\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeRequestExpired,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Request has expired\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"expired\", \"timestamp\", \"date\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with expired timestamps. AWS S3 requires requests to be made within a specific time window (typically 15 minutes).\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n\t},\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR PATTERNS\n// =============================================================================\n// These patterns are specifically designed for authentication-related testing.\n// They cover the spectrum of authentication failures from missing credentials\n// to expired timestamps.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthErrorPatterns provides predefined patterns for authentication error scenarios.\nvar AuthErrorPatterns = struct {\n\t// MissingAuthHeader covers requests without Authorization header\n\tMissingAuthHeader ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// InvalidAccessKeyId covers requests with invalid access key\n\tInvalidAccessKeyId ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// SignatureDoesNotMatch covers requests with incorrect signature\n\tSignatureDoesNotMatch ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// MissingDateHeader covers requests without X-Amz-Date header\n\tMissingDateHeader ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// RequestExpired covers requests with expired timestamp\n\tRequestExpired ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// MalformedAuthHeader covers requests with invalid Authorization header format\n\tMalformedAuthHeader ErrorScenarioConfig\n}{\n\t// MissingAuthHeader: No Authorization header present\n\tMissingAuthHeader: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Missing Authentication Token\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Missing Authentication Token\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"missing\", \"authentication\", \"token\", \"authorization\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    250 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests completely missing the Authorization header. This is the most basic authentication failure scenario.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n\t},\n\n\t// InvalidAccessKeyId: Access key not found or invalid\n\tInvalidAccessKeyId: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Invalid Access Key ID\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"access\", \"key\", \"invalid\", \"exist\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with an invalid or non-existent access key ID. This typically means the key was mistyped or does not belong to any account.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n\t},\n\n\t// SignatureDoesNotMatch: Calculated signature doesn't match\n\tSignatureDoesNotMatch: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Signature Does Not Match\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"signature\", \"match\", \"calculated\", \"provided\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   30,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    350 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests where the signature calculation failed. This can be due to incorrect secret key, tampered request, or signing algorithm mismatch.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n\t},\n\n\t// MissingDateHeader: Required date header is missing\n\tMissingDateHeader: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Missing Date Header\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Missing required date header\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"missing\", \"date\", \"header\", \"timestamp\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    250 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests missing the X-Amz-Date header. The date header is required for signature calculation and request timestamp validation.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n\t},\n\n\t// RequestExpired: Request timestamp is too old\n\tRequestExpired: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Request Expired\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeRequestExpired,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Request has expired\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"expired\", \"timestamp\", \"date\", \"old\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    300 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with timestamps outside the valid window (typically more than 15 minutes old). AWS S3 rejects stale requests.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n\t},\n\n\t// MalformedAuthHeader: Authorization header format is invalid\n\tMalformedAuthHeader: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Malformed Authorization Header\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    403,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Invalid authorization header format\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"authorization\", \"header\", \"malformed\", \"format\", \"invalid\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   20,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    250 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for requests with Authorization headers that don't match the expected AWS signature format. Covers missing fields or incorrect structure.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryAuth),\n\t},\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CLIENT ERROR PATTERNS (4xx)\n// =============================================================================\n// These patterns represent client-side errors (4xx status codes). These are\n// errors where the client made an invalid request and should correct their\n// request before retrying.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ClientErrorPatterns provides predefined patterns for client error scenarios (4xx).\nvar ClientErrorPatterns = struct {\n\t// BadRequest covers generic 400 Bad Request errors\n\tBadRequest ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// NotFound covers 404 Not Found errors\n\tNotFound ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// MethodNotAllowed covers 405 Method Not Allowed errors\n\tMethodNotAllowed ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// UnsupportedMediaType covers 415 Unsupported Media Type errors\n\tUnsupportedMediaType ErrorScenarioConfig\n}{\n\t// BadRequest: Generic bad request error\n\tBadRequest: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Bad Request\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInvalidRequest,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    400,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Bad Request\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"bad\", \"request\", \"invalid\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   10,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    200 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for generic bad request errors. This is a catch-all for malformed requests that don't fit more specific error categories.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInvalidRequest),\n\t},\n\n\t// NotFound: Resource not found\n\tNotFound: CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound,\n\n\t// MethodNotAllowed: HTTP method not supported\n\tMethodNotAllowed: CommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n\n\t// UnsupportedMediaType: Content type not supported\n\tUnsupportedMediaType: CommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SERVER ERROR PATTERNS (5xx)\n// =============================================================================\n// These patterns represent server-side errors (5xx status codes). These are\n// errors where the server failed to fulfill a valid request.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ServerErrorPatterns provides predefined patterns for server error scenarios (5xx).\nvar ServerErrorPatterns = struct {\n\t// InternalError covers 500 Internal Server Error\n\tInternalError ErrorScenarioConfig\n\n\t// ServiceUnavailable covers 503 Service Unavailable\n\tServiceUnavailable ErrorScenarioConfig\n}{\n\t// InternalError: Generic internal server error\n\tInternalError: CommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError,\n\n\t// ServiceUnavailable: Service temporarily unavailable\n\tServiceUnavailable: ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tName:              \"Service Unavailable\",\n\t\tExpectedCode:      ErrorCodeInternalError,\n\t\tExpectedStatus:    503,\n\t\tExpectedMessage:   \"Service Unavailable\",\n\t\tExpectedKeywords:  []string{\"unavailable\", \"service\", \"temporarily\"},\n\t\tMinMessageLength:   15,\n\t\tMaxResponseTime:    2000 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\tDescription:       \"Tests for service unavailability errors. This typically occurs during maintenance, overload, or infrastructure issues.\",\n\t\tCategory:          string(CategoryInternal),\n\t},\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR PATTERN COLLECTIONS BY CATEGORY\n// =============================================================================\n// These collections organize patterns by error category for easy access\n// when testing specific types of errors.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorPatternByCategory maps error categories to their predefined patterns.\n// This allows quick access to all patterns for a specific error type.\nvar ErrorPatternByCategory = map[ErrorCategory][]ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\tCategoryAuth: {\n\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader,\n\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.InvalidAccessKeyId,\n\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.SignatureDoesNotMatch,\n\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.MissingDateHeader,\n\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.RequestExpired,\n\t\tAuthErrorPatterns.MalformedAuthHeader,\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.SignatureMismatch,\n\t},\n\tCategoryNotFound: {\n\t\tClientErrorPatterns.NotFound,\n\t},\n\tCategoryInvalidRequest: {\n\t\tClientErrorPatterns.BadRequest,\n\t\tClientErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest,\n\t},\n\tCategoryMethodNotAllowed: {\n\t\tClientErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n\t},\n\tCategoryInternal: {\n\t\tServerErrorPatterns.InternalError,\n\t\tServerErrorPatterns.ServiceUnavailable,\n\t},\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR PATTERN BUILDERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These builder functions help create custom error patterns based on\n// predefined patterns or from scratch.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// PatternForCode returns a predefined pattern for the given error code,\n// or a default pattern if no specific pattern exists.\nfunc PatternForCode(code string) ErrorScenarioConfig {\n\tswitch code {\n\tcase ErrorCodeAccessDenied:\n\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied\n\tcase ErrorCodeInvalidAccessKeyId:\n\t\treturn AuthErrorPatterns.InvalidAccessKeyId\n\tcase ErrorCodeSignatureDoesNotMatch:\n\t\treturn AuthErrorPatterns.SignatureDoesNotMatch\n\tcase ErrorCodeMissingAuthenticationToken:\n\t\treturn AuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader\n\tcase ErrorCodeNoSuchKey:\n\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n\tcase ErrorCodeMethodNotAllowed:\n\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed\n\tcase ErrorCodeUnsupportedMediaType:\n\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType\n\tcase ErrorCodeInvalidRequest:\n\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest\n\tcase ErrorCodeInternalError:\n\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError\n\tcase ErrorCodeRequestExpired:\n\t\treturn CommonErrorPatterns.RequestExpired\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// Return a default pattern for unknown codes\n\t\treturn ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\t\tName:             \"Unknown Error Pattern\",\n\t\t\tExpectedCode:     code,\n\t\t\tExpectedStatus:   500,\n\t\t\tExpectedMessage:  \"Unknown error\",\n\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 10,\n\t\t\tMaxResponseTime:  1000 * time.Millisecond,\n\t\t\tDescription:      \"Default pattern for unknown error codes\",\n\t\t\tCategory:         string(CategoryGeneral),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// PatternsForCategory returns all predefined patterns for a given error category.\nfunc PatternsForCategory(category ErrorCategory) []ErrorScenarioConfig {\n\tpatterns, exists := ErrorPatternByCategory[category]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn []ErrorScenarioConfig{}\n\t}\n\tresult := make([]ErrorScenarioConfig, len(patterns))\n\tcopy(result, patterns)\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// AllCommonPatterns returns all predefined common error patterns.\nfunc AllCommonPatterns() []ErrorScenarioConfig {\n\treturn []ErrorScenarioConfig{\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound,\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.AccessDenied,\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.InvalidRequest,\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.UnsupportedMediaType,\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.MethodNotAllowed,\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.InternalServerError,\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.SignatureMismatch,\n\t\tCommonErrorPatterns.RequestExpired,\n\t}\n}\n\n// infrastructure\n- http_error_fixtures.go - HTTP error response fixtures\n\nUsage Patterns:\n\n1. Using predefined patterns:\n   pattern := CommonErrorPatterns.ResourceNotFound\n   config := pattern.Config()\n\n2. Getting a pattern by error code:\n   pattern := PatternForCode(\"NoSuchKey\")\n\n3. Getting all patterns for a category:\n   authPatterns := PatternsForCategory(CategoryAuth)\n\n4. Getting all common patterns:\n   allPatterns := AllCommonPatterns()\n\n5. Using auth-specific patterns:\n   pattern := AuthErrorPatterns.MissingAuthHeader\n   expectedStatus := pattern.ExpectedStatus\n\nPattern Categories:\n\n- CommonErrorPatterns: 8 patterns covering the most common S3 error scenarios\n- AuthErrorPatterns: 6 patterns specifically for authentication failures\n- ClientErrorPatterns: 4 patterns for 4xx client errors\n- ServerErrorPatterns: 2 patterns for 5xx server errors\n- ErrorPatternByCategory: Map-based access to patterns by category\n\nExtension Points:\n\n- Add new predefined patterns to the pattern structs\n- Extend ErrorCategory for new error types\n- Add new mapping functions for additional error metadata\n- Create custom fixture types by extending ErrorResponseFixture\n- Add builder functions for specific pattern types\n\nBest Practices:\n\n1.
